_comment: THE single machine-readable source of truth for Mental's supported platform matrix. Every consumer — core/build.gradle.kts task registration + plugin.yml api-version, scripts/integration-matrix.sh, .github/workflows/{build,release}.yml, the release-notes range — reads this file (jq / JsonSlurper). No Minecraft version or JDK literal may live anywhere else. Entries are version-sorted ascending, so entries[0] is the support floor (the legacy backport floor 1.9.4) and entries[-1] is the ceiling — the release-notes range and floor derivations read positionally. Paper entries drive the full era suites; the folia entry (platform: folia, suites: combat-smoke) boots a real Folia server and runs the boot suite + the Folia combat smoke. suites = full | boot | combat-smoke; a 'boot' entry runs only the classload/boot-safety suite (the legacy backport tier — feature correctness on those versions is promoted per-phase). serverFlags = extra JVM args prepended to the server boot; the legacy Paper builds from 1.13 up carry a soft Java-version guard that -DPaper.IgnoreJavaVersion=true bypasses (Java 17 runs them all). Absent serverFlags ⇒ none. NOTE: 1.14.4 is a KNOWN HOLE in the range — its terminal Paper build hard-caps at Java 13 with no IgnoreJavaVersion escape, so a Java-17-classfile plugin cannot load on any 1.14.4 server on a modern JVM (see docs/superpowers/research/2026-07-02-legacy-boot-viability.md); it is deliberately absent.

ocm._comment: The pinned kernitus OldCombatMechanics release the coexistence gate stages. CI (stageOcmJar) downloads url, verifies sha256, and stages to run/ocm-jar/OldCombatMechanics.jar. A locally-present jar there (e.g. a fork build) overrides the download. The OCM coexistence gate runs on the fixed 1.17.1 + 26.1.2 pair (its scope is the OCM ownership split, unchanged by the legacy backport) — pinned by version in the build, not positionally.
